Use Case Guide

Construction Software for Payroll

Construction payroll is complicated — multiple pay rates, certified payroll reports, multi-state workers, and union tracking. Here's how software handles it and what to look for.

✓ Updated April 2026

Construction payroll is more complex than payroll in most other industries. Workers often have multiple pay rates depending on the trade and work type they're performing, they may work across multiple jobs (and states) in a single pay period, and government contracts frequently require certified payroll reports in specific formats. Generic payroll software handles none of this well.

Construction companies typically approach payroll in one of three ways: construction-specific accounting software with built-in payroll, integration between construction management software and a payroll provider, or a dedicated payroll service with manual job cost tracking.

The construction payroll complexity factors

Multiple pay rates per worker

A carpenter might earn one rate when doing rough carpentry, another rate when doing finish work, and a third rate when operating equipment. Each hour on a single timecard might require a different rate. Generic payroll software treats employees as having one rate (or maybe a flat overtime rate). Construction-specific payroll handles multiple rate codes per worker per timecard period.

Certified payroll (prevailing wage)

Government-funded projects — federal contracts, many state projects, Davis-Bacon Act work — require contractors to pay prevailing wages by trade and to submit weekly certified payroll reports in WH-347 format. These reports document each worker's hours, trade classification, wage paid, and fringe benefits, certifying that prevailing wage requirements were met. Generating accurate WH-347 reports manually is a multi-hour task each week; purpose-built software generates them automatically.

Union fringe benefits

Union workers have fringe benefit contributions — health insurance, pension, training fund, vacation fund — specified in their collective bargaining agreement. These contributions vary by trade, union local, and sometimes by geographic area. Construction payroll software calculates and tracks these contributions automatically against the applicable CBA.

Multi-state payroll

Contractors working in multiple states face different tax withholding rules, prevailing wage rates, and reporting requirements in each state. Traveling workers who live in one state but work in another trigger split-state payroll calculations. Software that handles multi-state payroll correctly reduces compliance risk significantly.

Job cost allocation

Construction payroll needs to allocate labor costs to specific jobs and cost codes — not just to an employee's general wage expense. Payroll is one of the largest costs on most construction jobs, and accurate job cost reporting requires that each hour of labor be allocated to the correct project and cost category.

Software options for construction payroll

Construction accounting platforms with built-in payroll

Foundation Software and Sage 100 Contractor both include construction payroll modules that handle certified payroll, prevailing wages, union fringe benefits, multiple pay rates, and multi-state payroll natively. For mid-size contractors doing prevailing wage or union work, this integrated approach — where payroll data flows automatically into job cost reports — is the most accurate and efficient option. Both platforms require significant investment (several hundred dollars per month, plus implementation costs).

Construction management + payroll integration

Buildertrend integrates with several payroll providers including QuickBooks Payroll, ADP, and others. Field time tracking in Buildertrend can sync to these payroll providers, reducing double-entry. Buildertrend itself doesn't run payroll, but the integration handles the data flow from job site time capture to payroll processing. This approach works well for residential contractors who don't need certified payroll or union tracking.

Buildertrend — Integrates with QuickBooks Payroll and ADP for seamless time-to-payroll workflows.

Visit Buildertrend →

Raken captures field time cards with cost code allocation and integrates with several payroll and accounting platforms. If accurate field time capture is the bottleneck before payroll processing, Raken's mobile time tracking with voice entry is one of the fastest ways to get crew hours into the system accurately.

Dedicated payroll services for construction

Several payroll providers have built construction-specific capabilities:

  • ADP (Construction edition): ADP offers construction-specific payroll features including certified payroll reports, union deductions, and multiple pay rate handling. Integrates with several construction PM platforms.
  • Paylocity: HR and payroll platform with construction-specific configurations used by mid-size construction firms.
  • Gusto: Popular with small construction companies for its simple interface and QuickBooks integration. Handles basic multi-state payroll but lacks certified payroll and union tracking. Good for small residential contractors without prevailing wage complexity.
  • QuickBooks Payroll: Works for small to mid-size contractors without prevailing wage requirements. Integrates tightly with QuickBooks Online, which many small contractors use for accounting. Not suitable for certified payroll or union work.

What to look for in construction payroll software

  • Multiple pay rates per employee: Rate codes that change by trade, work type, or job
  • Certified payroll (WH-347) generation: Automatic report generation for prevailing wage jobs
  • Prevailing wage rate tables: Built-in or importable wage rates by trade and location
  • Union fringe benefit tracking: CBA-driven contribution calculations by trade and local
  • Multi-state payroll: Proper withholding for workers in multiple states
  • Job cost allocation: Labor costs allocated to project and cost code automatically
  • Time capture integration: Connect field time tracking (Buildertrend, Raken, Clockify) to avoid manual data entry
  • Direct deposit and tax filing

The right approach by company type

  • Small residential contractor, no prevailing wage: Gusto or QuickBooks Payroll + Buildertrend for time tracking
  • Mid-size contractor with prevailing wage or union work: Foundation Software or Sage 100 Contractor with built-in payroll
  • Commercial GC needing payroll + full PM: ADP (construction) integrated with your PM platform (Procore, Buildertrend)
  • Heavy field time capture priority: Raken for time tracking + your existing payroll platform

If you're currently running payroll manually or in spreadsheets, any of the above is a significant improvement. Start with what handles your specific payroll complexity — certified payroll and union tracking are the biggest differentiators to look for if they apply to your work.
